item.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire tested as U.S. guides ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Hegseth said the ceasefire with Iran "certainly holds" for now after a naval clash between Iranian and U.S. forces and renewed attacks on the UAE.

item.title: UnitedHealthcare to cut prior authorization for 30% of treatments
item.description: The largest U.S. health insurer said it will eliminate approval requirements for some treatments, including select outpatient surgeries and other procedures.

item.title: 2 hikers injured in bear attack at Yellowstone National Park
item.description: Two hikers were attacked by one or more bears, officials said, marking the first time in 2026 that a bear has injured people at Yellowstone.

item.title: Amid attacks by Trump, Justice Gorsuch says "my loyalty is to the Constitution"
item.description: President Trump has attacked Justices Neil Gorsuch and Amy Coney Barrett for voting to strike down his most sweeping tariffs.

item.title: See highlights from fashion's biggest night at Met Gala
item.description: Stars shined at fashion's biggest night of the year on Monday, the Met Gala. This year's event was co-chaired by Beyoncé, Nicole Kidman and Venus Williams. Outside the museum, protesters gathered holding letters that spelled out "tax the rich." Adriana Diaz reports.

item.title: Oprah selects "John of John" for latest book club pick
item.description: Oprah Winfrey exclusively announced on "CBS Mornings" her latest book club selection, "John of John" by Douglas Stuart, who called having his book picked by Winfrey "mind blowing."

item.title: Pennsylvania suing Character AI, claims chatbot posed as medical professional
item.description: A Character AI chatbot falsely claimed to be a licensed psychiatrist in Pennsylvania and provided an invalid license number, the state alleged.

item.title: Hantavirus possibly transmitted human to human on stricken cruise ship, WHO says
item.description: The WHO said Tuesday that Spain would allow the vessel to dock in the Canary Islands for a "full investigation" and "full disinfection."

item.title: For some older Americans, retirement today means unretiring
item.description: Nearly 20% of Americans of retirement age are employed or seeking employment. "You have to eat," said one 69-year-old of her reasons for continuing to work.

item.title: Dolly Parton shares health update after canceling Las Vegas residency
item.description: In a video posted to social media on Monday, Dolly Parton said she's been having immune and digestive issues, plus chronic kidney stones. Parton, who canceled a previously postponed Las Vegas residency, said her health is "improving every day" but she isn't performance-ready.

item.title: 2026 Tony Awards nominations are announced. See the full list.
item.description: Uzo Aduba and Darren Criss announced the 2026 Tony nominees for select categories on "CBS Mornings" on Tuesday.

item.title: Coinbase to lay off 700 workers as crypto company embraces AI
item.description: "This is a new way of working, and we need to leverage AI across every facet of our jobs," CEO Brian Armstrong said in a letter to employees.

item.title: Pentagon gives update on Iran war as Navy destroyers transit Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth and Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff Gen. Dan Caine held a presser at the Pentagon and discussed Project Freedom, a U.S. operation in the Strait of Hormuz. CBS News' James LaPorta and Ramy Inocencio have more.

item.title: Tony Awards nominees for 6 major categories exclusively revealed on "CBS Mornings"
item.description: "CBS Mornings" reveals the nominees for six major categories for the 79th annual Tony Awards, which air June 7 on CBS.

item.title: American stuck on hantavirus-stricken cruise ship speaks out: "All we want ... is to feel safe"
item.description: The World Health Organization said a cruise ship quarantined off West Africa will continue to Spain's Canary Islands after two passengers with the dangerous hantavirus are medically evacuated. Seven passengers are believed to have gotten the virus and three have died. Tom Hanson has more on the outbreak, and CBS News medical correspondent Dr. Céline Gounder joins to discuss what to know about hantavirus.

item.title: What to know about Tuesday's primaries in Ohio and Indiana
item.description: Voters are going to the polls Tuesday in Ohio and Indiana — in the Buckeye State, they'll be casting ballots in Senate, House and governor's primary races, while Hoosiers will weigh in on House and state races.

item.title: Blake Lively, Justin Baldoni reach settlement in "It Ends With Us" legal battle
item.description: Just weeks before they were set to go to trial, "It Ends With Us" co-stars Blake Lively and Justin Baldoni reached a settlement. The terms of the settlement have not been disclosed. Jericka Duncan reports.

item.title: Key highlights from the 2026 Met Gala
item.description: Beyoncé returned to the Met Gala and was joined by Blue Ivy and Jay-Z. Celebrities wore colorful, intricate garments, paying homage to the Met's exhibit "Costume Art" and the dress code "Fashion is Art." PEOPLE's Brittany Talarico joins CBS News with more highlights.

item.title: Justice Gorsuch says his loyalty is to "Constitution and laws" amid Trump Supreme Court attacks
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Neil Gorsuch told Jan Crawford his loyalty is to the "Constitution and laws of the United States," when asked if a justice owes a president loyalty following President Trump's attacks towards the high court. He also addressed the climate of today's Supreme Court and his new children's book.

item.title: Secret Service shoots gunman 4 blocks from White House
item.description: The White House briefly went on lockdown Monday after a man allegedly shot at Secret Service officers four blocks away, near the Washington Monument. Officers returned fire, hitting the suspect.

item.title: Key primary elections in Ohio and Indiana
item.description: Ohio and Indiana are holding primary elections as the 2026 midterms near. CBS News' Fin Gómez reports.

item.title: How Spirit Airlines' shutdown is impacting airports across the U.S.
item.description: The country's four biggest airlines have rebooked about 94,000 Spirit customers since the airline ceased operations on Saturday, according to the Department of Transportation. The closure is leaving some airports without daily air service. Kris Van Cleave reports.

item.title: Surging gas prices due to war with Iran could impact congressional races
item.description: Vice President JD Vance is headed to Iowa, where competitive races in at least three congressional districts this fall could impact control of the House. Both parties believe the state is up for grabs because of the economy and rising prices due to the war with Iran.

item.title: Tensions escalate in Strait of Hormuz as Project Freedom pins U.S. Navy against Iran attacks
item.description: All eyes are on the Strait of Hormuz, where the U.S. has been fending off Iranian attacks while the Navy helps commercial vessels seeking passage. CBS News' Holly Williams reports.

item.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire as Trump launches mission to guide ships through Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The U.S. and Iran have exchanged fire amid a fragile ceasefire. Iranian state media claims Iran's navy fired warning shots at American destroyers. Meanwhile, the U.S. military said it shot down Iranian cruise missiles and drones, and sank multiple Iranian small boats. Holly Williams reports.

item.title: Extended interview: Supreme Court Justice Neil Gorsuch
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Neil Gorsuch sat down with CBS News chief legal correspondent Jan Crawford to discuss his new children's book, "Heroes of 1776: The Story of the Declaration of Independence," why he tells young people they are the luckiest people alive "in the history of the world" and more.

item.title: Hegseth says "ceasefire is not over" after Strait of Hormuz exchange of fire
item.description: Iranian vessels fired on U.S. ships guiding vessels through the Strait of Hormuz, resulting in the U.S. sinking several small Iranian boats.

item.title: 1 missing U.S. soldier in Morocco was trying to save another, officials say
item.description: Search and rescue efforts were still underway Tuesday after two U.S. Army soldiers went missing in the ocean off Morocco's southern coast over the weekend while off duty during a training exercise.

item.title: Infamous drug lord pens letters from U.S. prison, citing "cruel punishment"
item.description: Joaquin "El Chapo" Guzman is serving a life sentence at a maximum security facility in Colorado.

item.title: 2 U.S. service members missing amid training exercise in Morocco
item.description: The service members were participating in African Lion, the largest joint military exercise on the continent.
